entries.notes: Manual review confirmed the Microsoft Learn page documents Game Mode as a real Windows feature area. The record uses it only for feature-level proof, not for the AutoGameModeEnabled registry mapping.

Manual review confirmed the support page directly documents the fullscreen/windowed-games feature area and user-facing toggle path. The record does not claim the page publishes the raw GameConfigStore tuple.

Manual review confirmed the support material is used only for the user-facing Restart apps sign-in feature. The record relies on VM runtime proof for the RestartApps registry mapping, not on the support page for raw registry semantics.
Manual review confirmed the Win32_OperatingSystem page is used narrowly for the path plus high-level WMI mapping to Win32PrioritySeparation, not for raw 0x26 bit semantics.
Manual review confirmed the MMCSS page is used only for the SystemResponsiveness path plus rounding/clamping behavior. It is not used as proof of NetworkThrottlingIndex semantics.
Manual review confirmed the validation proof points only to the SystemResponsiveness path plus rounding/clamping portion of the MMCSS page.

Manual review confirmed the Microsoft Q&A article contains the same add/delete CLSID workaround commands used in the record. The record still treats the workaround as potentially deprecated on newer 24H2-era builds.
Manual review confirmed the VS Code default settings reference is being used only to prove the documented git.autofetch settings surface, not the app's unrelated Explorer registry write.

Manual review confirmed the Source Control FAQ explicitly discusses disabling autofetch through git.autofetch. The record uses this page for the VS Code setting only, not for the app's registry mapping.
Manual review confirmed the FAQ line about git.autofetch matches the validation proof claim in this deprecated record.

Manual review confirmed Microsoft's DirectX post directly documents Hardware-Accelerated GPU Scheduling as a real Windows graphics feature and user-facing settings area. The record uses it for feature-level proof, not as the raw HwSchMode registry contract.

Manual review confirmed Docker's settings page documents the Windows settings-store.json location. The record uses this page for the supported file-backed settings surface rather than for registry semantics.
Manual review confirmed Docker's settings reference documents wslEngineEnabled as the WSL 2 engine setting. The record uses it for the documented file-backed Docker settings model.
Manual review confirmed the settings reference includes the WSL engine wording cited in the validation proof.

Manual review confirmed Python's Windows documentation explicitly points readers to Enable Win32 long paths or LongPathsEnabled=1 in the FileSystem registry key. The record uses this page for the Python workflow context.
Manual review confirmed the exact quote in the validation proof matches the current Python Windows documentation wording about LongPathsEnabled.
Manual review confirmed Microsoft directly documents LongPathsEnabled under HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\FileSystem and states that value 1 enables long paths for compatible applications.
Manual review confirmed the validation proof quote matches the current Microsoft path-length documentation for LongPathsEnabled.
Manual review confirmed Microsoft's long-path page is a correct exact-path and exact-value source for LongPathsEnabled in this record.
Manual review confirmed Microsoft's long-path documentation matches this record's narrow claim: LongPathsEnabled is the documented Windows control for enabling long paths for compatible applications.
Manual review confirmed this note references the same Microsoft long-path page in a way that matches the page content.
Manual review confirmed the Start Policy CSP directly documents HideRecommendedPersonalizedSites, including user scope, Explorer policy path, and 0/1 meanings.
Manual review confirmed the Start Policy CSP directly documents HideRecommendedPersonalizedSites, including device scope, Explorer policy path, and 0/1 meanings.
Manual review confirmed the Start Policy CSP directly documents HideRecommendedSection for user scope with the same Explorer policy path and 0/1 semantics used by the record.
Manual review confirmed the Start Policy CSP directly documents HideRecommendedSection for device scope with the same Explorer policy path and 0/1 semantics used by the record.
Manual review confirmed the validation proof quote for HideRecommendedSection matches the current Start Policy CSP wording.

Manual review confirmed the TDR registry page documents TdrDelay, TdrDdiDelay, TdrLevel, TdrLimitCount, and TdrLimitTime, but it does not publish DisableOverlays. This remains a nearest-surface reference rather than a fit-for-claim source.
Manual review confirmed the TDR registry page does not publish PageFaultDebugMode. The record can keep the page as adjacent provenance, but not as a direct context-fit source.
Manual review confirmed the TDR page directly documents TdrDdiDelay with the exact GraphicsDrivers path and default value.
Manual review confirmed the validation proof quote for TdrDdiDelay matches the current Microsoft TDR registry page.
Manual review confirmed the TDR page directly documents TdrDelay with the exact GraphicsDrivers path and the documented default of 2 seconds.
Manual review confirmed the validation proof quote for TdrDelay matches the current Microsoft TDR registry page.
Manual review confirmed the TDR page directly documents TdrLevel with the exact GraphicsDrivers path and the recovery-mode value table.
Manual review confirmed the validation proof quote for TdrLevel matches the current Microsoft TDR registry page.
Manual review confirmed the TDR page directly documents TdrLimitCount with the exact GraphicsDrivers path and documented default.
Manual review confirmed the validation proof quote for TdrLimitCount matches the current Microsoft TDR registry page.
Manual review confirmed the TDR page directly documents TdrLimitTime with the exact GraphicsDrivers path and documented default.
Manual review confirmed the validation proof quote for TdrLimitTime matches the current Microsoft TDR registry page.
Manual review confirmed fsutil behavior directly documents disablelastaccess and the matching NtfsDisableLastAccessUpdate registry mapping.
Manual review confirmed fsutil behavior documents memoryusage and the matching NtfsMemoryUsage registry mapping.
Manual review confirmed the validation proof for NtfsMemoryUsage matches the current fsutil behavior documentation.
Manual review confirmed fsutil behavior documents mftzone and its supported value range as the source used by this record.
Manual review confirmed the note references the current fsutil behavior page in a way that matches the documented NTFS controls.
Manual review confirmed the IPv6 guidance directly documents DisabledComponents under Tcpip6\Parameters and the 0xFF disable value.
Manual review confirmed the validation proof quote for disabling IPv6 matches the current Microsoft IPv6 guidance.
Manual review confirmed the IPv6 guidance directly documents DisabledComponents = 0x20 for preferring IPv4 over IPv6.
Manual review confirmed the validation proof quote for preferring IPv4 matches the current Microsoft IPv6 guidance.
Manual review confirmed this note references the current Microsoft IPv6 configuration guidance in a way that matches the page content.
Manual review confirmed the Microsoft Q&A page includes the exact quoted NonPagedPoolSize=0 line used as the default reset capture in this legacy record.
Manual review confirmed this Q&A page is being used narrowly as a captured default-registry snapshot that includes NonPagedPoolSize=0, not as a broad modern memory-management specification.
Manual review confirmed the Microsoft Q&A page includes the exact quoted PagedPoolSize=0 line used as the default reset capture in this legacy record.
Manual review confirmed this Q&A page is being used narrowly as a captured default-registry snapshot that includes PagedPoolSize=0, not as a broad modern memory-management specification.

Manual review confirmed the Memory Management Registry Keys page discusses current documented pool-limit surfaces but does not directly publish NonPagedPoolSize as a modern exact-fit source for this record.

Manual review confirmed the Memory Management Registry Keys page discusses current documented pool-limit surfaces but does not directly publish PagedPoolSize as a modern exact-fit source for this record.
Manual review confirmed the note references this page as a broad memory-management lead rather than as an exact direct mapping for the legacy pool-size values.
Manual review confirmed the Microsoft Q&A answer includes the exact Shell Icons value 29 override and resource path quoted in the validation proof.
Manual review confirmed this Microsoft Q&A page is used as medium-strength product guidance for the exact Shell Icons value 29 shortcut-arrow override.

Manual review confirmed the RM_SHUTDOWN_TYPE page is used narrowly for the documented relationship between forced shutdown behavior and the system timeout concepts, not as a direct registry-path source.
Manual review confirmed the shutdown note references the Restart Manager page in a way that matches the page's forced-shutdown timeout discussion.
